summary:
Self-reported health-related quality of life is not established as an independent prognostic factor for survival in non-small-cell lung cancer; instead, it serves as a proxy for validated measures like performance status and weight loss. The current evidence does not demonstrate that these reported outcomes predict mortality on their own but only through association with recognized prognostic elements. 

Note: This summary focuses exclusively on the core clinical findings from the provided text without adding external interpretation.
